seoTitle: Port Kenny vs Port Macdonnell — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Kenny and Port Macdonnell. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Kenny vs Port Macdonnell: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Kenny and Port Macdonnell.
heading: Port Kenny vs Port Macdonnell — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Kenny, South Australia and Port Macdonnell, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Macdonnell has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Port Kenny (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Kenny or Port Macdonnell safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Kenny has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Port Macdonnell scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Kenny scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Port Macdonnell scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Kenny or Port Macdonnell?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Macdonnell has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
